ddls:
CREATE TABLE "neighborhoods" (
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each neighborhood */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the neighborhood */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the neighborhood */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the neighborhood (e.g., downtown, uptown) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the neighborhood */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the neighborhood in square miles */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "business_types" (
  "business_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each business type */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the business type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the business type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the business type (e.g., food, retail) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("business_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "businesses" (
  "business_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each business */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the business */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the business */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the business */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the business */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the business */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("business_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "neighborhood_businesses" (
  "neighborhood_business_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each neighborhood business */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood the business belongs to */,
  "business_id" INTEGER /* ID of the business */,
  "business_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the business type */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the business type in the neighborhood (e.g., most common, 2nd most common) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("neighborhood_business_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_neighborhood_businesses_business_id FOREIGN KEY ("business_id") REFERENCES businesses ("business_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_neighborhood_businesses_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_neighborhood_businesses_business_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("business_type_id") REFERENCES business_types ("business_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "business_frequency" (
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each frequency */,
  "neighborhood_business_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood business */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the business type in the neighborhood */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the frequency was observed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_business_frequency_neighborhood_business_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_business_id") REFERENCES neighborhood_businesses ("neighborhood_business_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., city planner, business analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the platform was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "neighborhood_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood the trend belongs to */,
  "business_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the business type */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the trend was observed */,
  "trend" TEXT /* Description of the trend (e.g., increase, decrease, stable) */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Growth rate of the trend (e.g., 10%, 5%) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_neighborhood_trends_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_neighborhood_trends_business_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("business_type_id") REFERENCES business_types ("business_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "business_concentration" (
  "concentration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each concentration */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood the concentration belongs to */,
  "business_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the business type */,
  "concentration" REAL /* Measure of business concentration (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "density" REAL /* Density of businesses in the neighborhood (e.g., businesses per square mile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("concentration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_business_concentration_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_business_concentration_business_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("business_type_id") REFERENCES business_types ("business_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., trend, concentration) */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Binary data for the report (e.g., PDF, Excel) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
